title: Kung Pao Chicken

ingredients.title: Chicken Marinade
items: 500 g (1 lb) boneless, skinless chicken thighs, diced into 1.5 cm cubes

ingredients.title: Sauce
items: 1 tsp cornstarch, mixed with 2 tsp water (slurry)

ingredients.title: Aromatics & Main Dish
items: 8–10 dried red chilies (facing heaven chilies preferred), deseeded for milder heat
4 scallions, white and green parts separated (white chopped into 2 cm pieces)
3 tbsp neutral oil (peanut or grapeseed oil recommended)

instruction.title: Step 1: Marinate the Chicken
steps: Mix chicken with Shaoxing wine, soy sauces, cornstarch, and sesame oil.
Let rest 15–20 minutes for flavor absorption.

instruction.title: Step 2: Prepare the Sauce
steps: In a small bowl, whisk soy sauces, vinegar, Shaoxing wine, and sugar.
Set aside the cornstarch slurry separately for thickening at the end.

instruction.title: Step 3: Aromatic Oil Infusion
steps: Heat wok over medium-high until just smoking.
Add oil, then chilies and Sichuan peppercorns. Stir-fry until fragrant (10–15 seconds), being careful not to burn.

instruction.title: Step 4: Stir-Fry the Chicken
steps: Add marinated chicken. Stir-fry 3–4 minutes until browned and nearly cooked.
Push chicken to the sides; add garlic, ginger, and scallion whites. Stir until aromatic.

instruction.title: Step 5: Sauce & Finish
steps: Pour in sauce mixture. Toss until chicken is fully coated.
Stir in cornstarch slurry to thicken glaze (30–60 seconds).
Add peanuts and scallion greens, tossing everything quickly.

instruction.title: Step 6: Serve
steps: Transfer immediately to a heated serving plate.
Serve with steamed jasmine rice or hand-pulled noodles.

chefTips: Chili Control: For a true Sichuan kick, leave chili seeds in. For balance, remove them.
Peanuts: Roast fresh for best flavor; cashews may substitute for a more delicate crunch.
Wok Hei: Use high heat and quick motions to achieve that smoky, restaurant-level finish.
Balance: Adjust sugar and vinegar for your preferred sweet-sour ratio.
